title: The 'Star Wars' Movie Draft
format: A Star Wars draft (Sean, Mallory Rubin, Van Lathan, Amanda, Chris) across seven categories — Movie, Movie You're Cursed With, Skywalker Family Member, Hero or Villain, Vehicle, Problematic Character, and Wild Card. Only two of those categories are films, so rather than reconstruct the character-and-ship picks, here's the Star Wars filmography as the show ranked, cursed, and fought over it.

segments.heading: The films
segments.note: the Star Wars filmography, as the show sees it

films.title: The Empire Strikes Back
films.note: Sean's #1 — 'the greatest sequel ever made,' better than Godfather II. Made darker and better than it was ever mapped to be.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: A New Hope — Mallory's pick. 'It changed movies, changed genre films, and changed the history of story.' The binary sunset.

films.title: Return of the Jedi
films.note: The original trilogy's redemptive close — Vader's turn is the emotional core the whole saga orbits.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
films.note: 'A terrible movie,' but formative to the fandom — and Amanda's crash-course homework.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode II - Attack of the Clones
films.note: The weakest of the prequels — perennially conflated with the Clone Wars.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode III - Revenge of the Sith
films.note: 'Genuinely, unironically great' — the tragedy of Anakin finally lands.

films.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
films.note: The sequel trilogy's confident, if familiar, restart.

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: The great divide — 'a healthy debate about a Star Wars movie is totally valid,' but Lucasfilm then walked it back.

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: 'A shameful manifestation of fearing your own fanbase.' Somehow, Palpatine returned.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Home to the Vader hallway — 'exactly what I wanted to see when I was six.'

films.title: Star Wars: The Clone Wars
films.note: The animated theatrical film — 'pretty bad,' though the series it launched is among the best Star Wars ever.

films.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu
films.note: The upcoming film — 'I hope it's good and they let Grogu shine.'

films.title: Star Wars: Starfighter
films.note: Next May — Ryan Gosling, directed by Shawn Levy. 'The biggest star in the world.'
